page.content: The analysis of the data presented in Table 1 highlights significant findings related to the prevalence of large pulmonary embolism (PE) across different groups. Notably, the odds ratio (OR) for Group 3 indicates a substantial association with large PE, reported at 5.974 with a 95% confidence interval (CI) spanning from 1.811 to 19.703. This suggests that individuals in this group are nearly six times more likely to experience large PE compared to the reference cohort, with a statistically significant P-value of .003, underscoring the robustness of this association. Furthermore, the mean value for this group is recorded at 836.60 ± 48.97, reflecting not only the central tendency but also the variability of the data.

page.content: The range of observed values from 776 to 934 provides additional context for the distribution of the measurements within Group 3. These results emphasize the need for targeted clinical interventions and further research to understand the underlying mechanisms contributing to the increased risk of large PE in this population.
